title: onobrychis arenaria subsp. cana
intro: Onobrychis arenaria subsp. cana, also known as sainfoin, is a perennial legume that grows in dry, sunny areas and is native to Europe and Asia. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds, and has been traditionally used to treat a variety of health issues, such as digestive problems and skin irritations. The sainfoin plant is also valued for its health benefits, including its ability to lower cholesterol levels and improve digestion, as well as its potential to prevent certain types of cancer. Herbal preparations of sainfoin can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
